islands.name: Venture Foundry
islands.description: An industrial innovation district where aspiring entrepreneurs learn to launch, fund, and scale a business from scratch.

learning_objectives: Understand the steps from business idea to minimum viable product
Learn revenue models: subscription, per-unit, freemium, and service fees
Evaluate funding options: bootstrapping, loans, and equity investment
provenance.originality_notes: Theme: industrial foundry district with workshops and pitching stages (not a corporate boardroom or office tycoon). Verb: negotiate with NPC investors and allocate startup budget across departments (not passive empire building). Structure: milestone-based quest chain where each quest represents a startup phase — idea, build, fund, scale (not sandbox or elimination). Combines trade-negotiation with budget-allocation through original NPC mentorship scenarios.

areas.name: The Workshop
areas.description: A gritty maker space with 3D printers, whiteboards, and prototype stations. Ideas become real here.

areas.name: Pitch Stage
areas.description: A small theater where founders present their ideas to mentors and investors. Bright lights, big stakes.

areas.name: Growth Lab
areas.description: Screens show revenue dashboards and customer metrics. Scale-up strategists work here around the clock.

items.name: Business Plan
items.description: A one-page plan covering your idea, target customer, and how you'll make money.

items.name: Seed Funding
items.description: Your first round of capital. Use it wisely — it has to last until revenue starts flowing.

items.name: Profit & Loss Sheet
items.description: A simple P&L that shows whether your venture is making or losing money.

items.name: Foundry Badge
items.description: Awarded to entrepreneurs who complete the full startup journey from idea to growth.

quests.title: Idea to Plan
quests.description: Meet Founder Fern in the Workshop to brainstorm a business idea, then work with Mentor Malik to turn it into a one-page business plan.
quests.hint: Start at The Workshop. Talk to Fern for inspiration, then Malik for structure.

quests.title: Pitch and Grow
quests.description: Take your business plan to the Pitch Stage and convince Investor Ike to fund you. Then enter the Growth Lab to learn about scaling.
quests.hint: You need a Business Plan before pitching. Head to Pitch Stage, then Growth Lab.

nodes.speaker: Founder Fern
nodes.text: Every great business starts with a problem worth solving. What bugs you? What could be better? That frustration is your starting point!

choices.text: I have an idea! Help me develop it.

nodes.speaker: Founder Fern
nodes.text: Awesome! First question: who is your customer? Second: what problem do you solve for them? Third: how will you make money? Answer those three and you've got the bones of a business. Go see Mentor Malik to write it up!

nodes.speaker: Founder Fern
nodes.text: No rush! Walk around the Workshop, look at what others are building, and let inspiration strike.

nodes.speaker: Mentor Malik
nodes.text: A business plan doesn't need to be 50 pages. One page is enough: your idea, your customer, your revenue model, and your first-year costs. Let's draft yours.

nodes.speaker: Mentor Malik
nodes.text: Here's your Business Plan template. Fill in the blanks, then try the Startup Budget minigame to practice allocating your first funds. After that, you're ready to pitch!

nodes.speaker: Mentor Malik
nodes.text: A revenue model is HOW you make money. Do customers pay per item? A monthly subscription? A one-time fee? Each model has trade-offs. Pick the one that fits your customer best.

nodes.speaker: Investor Ike
nodes.text: So you want funding? I've heard a thousand pitches. Show me your Business Plan and tell me: what makes YOUR idea worth betting on?

choices.text: Here's my plan — it solves a real problem!

nodes.speaker: Investor Ike
nodes.text: I like it. Clear problem, clear customer, clear revenue path. Here's your Seed Funding — use it wisely! Head to the Growth Lab to learn how to scale without burning through cash.

nodes.speaker: Investor Ike
nodes.text: Three things: a real problem, a clear customer, and a plan to make money. If you can show me all three, I'll invest. If not, go back to the Workshop and refine your idea.

nodes.speaker: Strategist Sara
nodes.text: Congratulations on getting funded! Now the hard part: growing without running out of money. Revenue minus expenses equals profit. Let me show you how to track it.

choices.text: Teach me about profit and loss!
What's the biggest mistake new businesses make?

nodes.speaker: Strategist Sara
nodes.text: Here's your P&L sheet. Revenue at the top, subtract all costs, and the bottom line is your profit — or loss. Keep this positive and you'll survive. Keep it growing and you'll thrive!

nodes.speaker: Strategist Sara
nodes.text: Spending too fast. They get funding and hire everyone, buy everything. Smart founders keep costs low until revenue is steady. Grow lean!

minigames.name: Startup Budget
minigames.description: Allocate a limited seed budget across product development, marketing, operations, and savings. Aim for a balanced spend that survives 12 simulated months.

minigames.name: Price Your Product
minigames.description: Launch day! Slide your product's price and watch demand and profit react live. Discover the profit-maximizing price.
